mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls signal broad economic strength and robust corporate earnings growth, leading to positive equity market returns as investors price in higher future profits.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC labor market recovery has been a key driver of the bull market.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) is consistently negative over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: After a long period at the zero lower bound, any increase in the Federal Funds Rate signals the start of a tightening cycle. This increases the discount rate for future earnings and tightens financial conditions, causing negative forward returns for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed initiated its first rate hike in December 2015.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in the 60 trading days following more than 50% of instances where the FEDFUNDS rate increases by more than 0.10 over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in retail sales growth and an uptick in the unemployment rate indicates a sharp contraction in consumer health. This interaction is a leading indicator of recession and corporate earnings decline, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: This combined signal fails to precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 2 out of 3 consecutive triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month contraction in industrial production points to weakening manufacturing and business investment, which are leading indicators of a broader economic slowdown, negatively impacting equity market sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production has shown weakness in early 2016.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically different from the average return on non-trigger days (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below the estimated natural rate (NAIRU, proxied here as 5.0%), it signals a tight labor market. This leads to wage pressures, compresses corporate profit margins, and anticipates hawkish Fed policy, all of which are negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is currently at 5.0%, a post-crisis low.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0%, the S&P 500's average 60-day forward return is positive and greater than its long-term average.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation and a very low unemployment rate creates a 'policy mistake' risk. Investors anticipate the Federal Reserve will be forced to tighten policy more aggressively than expected to control inflation, causing a negative repricing of equities.
failure_condition: The model's Sharpe ratio over a 5-year walk-forward test is less than 0.1, indicating no predictive power after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and balance sheets, creating positive wealth effects and stimulating broader economic activity. This bullish signal for the real economy translates to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in HOUST and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding a high threshold signals an overheating economy. This leads market participants to price in imminent and aggressive monetary tightening from the central bank, causing a negative re-rating of equity valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: In periods following a GDP growth trigger, the 60-day forward returns of the S&P 500 are positive more than 60% of the time.
